overview: Processes XML documents using a DOM-based parser with full **ITS (Internationalization Tag Set)** support (both 1.0 and 2.0). By default, all element content is translatable and no attribute values are translatable, following ITS defaults. Behavior is customized through ITS rules files (`.fprm`) that define translatability, inline elements, ID values, white-space handling, and more. For very large XML documents without ITS requirements, consider the **XML Stream Filter** instead, as this filter loads the entire document into memory.

path.help:
Path to an **ITS rules file** (`.fprm`) that controls extraction behavior — which elements/attributes are translatable, inline codes, white-space handling, etc.

Without a rules file, ITS defaults apply:
- All element **content** is translatable
- No **attribute values** are translatable

The rules file is an XML document using ITS namespace `http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its`. Filter-specific options use the `okapi-framework:xmlfilter-options` namespace.

See the Okapi wiki article *"How to Create a Custom Configuration for the XML Filter"* for a walkthrough.
path.values: Path to an ITS-format XML file (`.fprm`). The file defines `<its:rules>` with ITS data categories and optional Okapi filter extensions.
notes: Filter options (`<okp:options>`) **must** be placed in the parameters file, not in embedded or linked ITS rules — options in embedded/linked rules have no effect.
The `xml:id` attribute takes precedence over `itsx:idValue` declarations when both are present on an element.
The `xml:space` attribute takes precedence over the `itsx:whiteSpaces` extension.

simplifierRules.help: Defines rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted segments. Simplifier rules reduce complex inline markup to simpler placeholders, making segments easier for translators to work with.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes that appear at the very start or end of a segment are often structural markup (e.g., wrapping `...`) rather than meaningful inline content. Moving them to the skeleton removes them from the translatable text, reducing clutter for translators while preserving them in the output.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other (e.g., `` or a closing tag immediately followed by an opening tag), they are combined into a single placeholder. This simplifies segments by reducing the number of codes translators must manage.

limitations: The ITS `withinTextRule` with value `nested` may behave as `yes` in some cases.
`xml:lang` attribute values are **not** updated to reflect the target language in output.
The entire input file is loaded into memory (DOM-based) — very large documents may cause memory issues. Use the **XML Stream Filter** for large files.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 regardless of any externally specified default encoding.
**Output BOM**: When output is UTF-8 and input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If input was a different encoding, no BOM is added.
**XML declaration**: Always written in output (with updated encoding) unless suppressed via `omitXMLDeclaration` option in the rules file. Added automatically even if the original lacked one.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the input.
**Attribute quoting**: All attributes in output use double-quotes. Double-quotes inside attribute values are always escaped; single-quotes are never escaped.

examples.title: ITS rules file with filter options
examples.description: A `.fprm` parameters file combining ITS translation rules with Okapi-specific filter options. Filter options use the `okapi-framework:xmlfilter-options` namespace and must be in the parameters file (not embedded in the XML).
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules version="1.0"
 xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its"
 xmlns:okp="okapi-framework:xmlfilter-options">
 <okp:options lineBreakAsCode="yes"
              escapeQuotes="no"
              escapeGT="yes"
 />
 <its:translateRule selector="//text" translate="yes"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//desc" translate="yes"/>
</its:rules>
```

examples.title: Code finder rules for inline patterns
examples.description: Use `<okp:codeFinder>` to define regex patterns that match variable placeholders or embedded markup within translatable text. Patterns must be XML-escaped. The `#v1` header, `count.i`, and `ruleN` format is required.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules version="1.0"
 xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its"
 xmlns:okp="okapi-framework:xmlfilter-options">
 <okp:codeFinder useCodeFinder="yes">#v1
count.i=2
rule0=<(/?)(\w+)[^>]*?>
rule1=(#\w+?\#)|(%\d+?%)
 </okp:codeFinder>
</its:rules>
```

examples.title: ITS idValue for custom resource names
examples.description: Use `itsx:idValue` with an XPath expression to assign unique resource names to text units. Useful when sibling elements share a parent ID and need disambiguation.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules version="1.0"
 xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its"
 xmlns:itsx="http://www.w3.org/2008/12/its-extensions">
 <its:translateRule selector="//text" translate="yes"
  itsx:idValue="concat(../@name, '_t')"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//desc" translate="yes"
  itsx:idValue="concat(../@name, '_d')"/>
</its:rules>
```
examples.output:
```
Resource name for <text>: id1_t
Resource name for <desc>: id1_d
```

examples.title: Extract untranslatable content with exclusions
examples.description: Extract `its:translate="no"` content for context, but exclude specific elements using `localeFilterList="!*"` as a workaround.
